action.reasoning: Observation: Current best LPIPS=0.6021 (iter16 partial fallback orbit); latest custom per-target rays (rel_az + offset from best_offset.txt=0) fails with consistent 'mat1 and mat2 shapes cannot be multiplied (7680x64 and 120x64)' on all offsets/objs in val_eval (likely wrong rays shape passed to renderer causing internal matmul on triplane feat=64 vs decoder hidden=~120; 7680=#points in chunk). Workspace predict.py/val_eval.py have robust get_spherical_cameras_with_azimuth, get_rgb_from_output, tensor_to_rgba_pil, white-BG composite but truncated/incomplete extraction for scene_codes->triplane and no flatten on rays. White-BG+RGB conversion and per-obj mean elev/dist not fully leveraged. Analysis: Matmul indicates renderer(decoder, triplane, rays_o, rays_d) receives mismatched rays shape (keepdim=True gives [1,H,W,3] vs expected flat [N,3]); flattening rays_o/rays_d.view(-1,3) + explicit contiguous + smaller chunk_size=8192 + scene_codes handling (assume tensor or ['triplane']) + per-view gc/empty_cache will fix. Custom pose match (rel_az=(target_az-input_az+offset)%360) is optimal for this task (zero-shot TripoSR <1B params, exact camera alignment beats default orbit mismatch on varying ~185° rel az). With 4 iters/7h left, fix+run val_eval for reliable best_offset (expect LPIPS<0.55), port to predict.py, test val, submit_eval. No training (time/model size).
action.command: python val_eval.py && python predict.py --data_path /data/val --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_predictions.json --num_objects 20
